key_event.1885-1894: Leopold II establishes Congo Free State as private colony
key_event.1895-1904: Rubber-quota terror and Force Publique consolidate despotic extraction
key_event.1905-1914: Belgian annexation institutionalizes extractive Despotic Leviathan (1908)
key_event.1915-1924: WWI mobilization, extractive boom, Kimbangu suppression
key_event.1925-1934: Consolidated extractive colonial state; Pende Revolt crushed
key_event.1935-1944: WWII "war effort": intensified extraction, forced labor, crushed strikes
key_event.1945-1954: Postwar boom, urbanization, and first legal African associations
key_event.1955-1964: Independence, Force Publique mutiny, secessions, and state collapse
key_event.1965-1974: Mobutu's 1965 coup and single-party despotic consolidation
key_event.1975-1984: Copper crash and Shaba invasions hollow out the state
key_event.1985-1994: Economic collapse, army pillages, and multiparty/civic mobilization
key_event.1995-2004: Two Congo Wars; state collapse into "Africa's World War"
key_event.2005-2014: 2006 elections and Kabila consolidation amid persistent eastern conflict
key_event.2015-2024: Church-led mobilization, disputed transfers, and M23 resurgence
